extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN won a chaotic sprint after late crash disrupted leadouts, beating KITTEL and EWAN in tight finish
tactical_patterns: GROENEWEGEN crash in final kilometer forced sprinter teams to reorganize without structured leadouts, favoring opportunistic positioning
Quick-Step delivered JAKOBSEN without visible leadout train, suggesting pre-positioned final 300m strategy rather than traditional pilot
Trek-Segafredo had THEUNS leading out but lost KITTEL early, revealing fragile German sprint infrastructure compared Belgian teams

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: won from chaos without apparent leadout, shows tactical maturity to capitalize when structured trains collapse
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: negative: had positioning through THEUNS but couldn't finish, suggests declining ability to navigate disrupted sprints
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: neutral: reached podium despite Mitchelton-Scott having no visible leadout support in final 500m
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: crashed in crucial final kilometer positioning phase, likely mechanical positioning error rather than contact
rider_signals.ŠTYBAR Zdeněk: neutral: present congratulating JAKOBSEN suggests Quick-Step used Classics riders for positioning rather than pure sprinter pilots
